title: Unit 01 versus Unit 03 question
wikitext:
[]: Unit 01 versus Unit 03 question             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Fri Oct 28, 2011 9:34 pm Why didn't Shinji just yank the plug out of Unit 03? When Unit 01 was ripping 03 apart, it didn't seem to have any trouble tearing the entry plug out with it's mouth. You could argue that it might mentally cripple Asuka somehow but by that time, it seems to look like a better option then destroying Unit 03 with Asuka still inside. And why didn't daddy Gendo think of that either?

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Fri Oct 28, 2011 10:03 pm The plug had goo on it. The goo was the angel. Which I'm not real sure how biting it killed it, or how that didn't kill Asuka. But whatever. The question I would have since they care so much about samples and whatnot is why didn't they try anything to capture Unit 03 intact? Like Unit 03 goes viral and two seconds later they're ready to consider it scrap. You mean they're not going to consider dropping bakelite or setting up some kind of trap before just ripping it into pieces? It only cost twenty bazillion dollars and is carrying a high profile Nerv pilot. It seems to me that the incident would create some international tensions with Unit 03 being the United States' last Eva and Asuka being Euro's poster child. Eh, Shinji, you should just kill it. Edit: And before anyone says, "Oh, but Brainman, they didn't do that in the old show either." I know. It was weird then too.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Fri Oct 28, 2011 10:08 pm Brainman wrote: The plug had goo on it. The goo was the angel. Which I'm not real sure how biting it killed it, or how that didn't kill Asuka. But whatever. The question I would have since they care so much about samples and whatnot is why didn't they try anything to capture Unit 03 intact? Like Unit 03 goes viral and two seconds later they're ready to consider it scrap. You mean they're not going to consider dropping bakelite or setting up some kind of trap before just ripping it into pieces? It only cost twenty bazillion dollars and is carrying a high profile Nerv pilot. Eh, Shinji, you should just kill it.

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Fri Oct 28, 2011 10:22 pm KnightmareX13 wrote: The angel turned the plug into it's core, so by crushing the plug you crush it's core. And the problem with capturing unit 03 is that it is moving and will attack any units that come close to it, and to study it would require removing some bakelite, which could allow it to escape. Nerv probably also learned a bit of lesson from the Tunniel incident about keeping angels locked up for dissection.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Oct 29, 2011 8:03 am Well, what's even stranger for me is that it took such an ungodly long time for Nerv to figure out that Eva-03 was infected by the Angel and it now poses a threat. They only confirmed Eva-03 as a hostile when it was already near the command center, which must have took some time, since the test was started in broad daylight and it's already sunset* when Bardiel arrives at the scene. I guess this could be explained away with Nerv's communication lines being in shambles after the explosion at Matsushiro, but it's still weird. As for why didn't they try dropping bakelite: maybe they just don't have the infrastructure? I don't think Nerv had secret bakelite pumps installed on the road between the Geofront and the Test Site, and even they probably don't have an airborne vehicle that is big enough to carry sufficient quantites of bakelite, can be fired up quickly, and can drop said quantity in a relatively small target area. And i can't really think of any other means to try and trap it. What, lure it in an Eva-sized hole with a giant Scooby Snack? Throw a giant fishing net at it? And by the time Eva-03 is facing off with Eva-01, it's already too late. Bardiel was only slow moving while approaching (because it's menacing), but in combat, he could do the same lightning acrobatics as any Eva. *Thoguh that could happen pretty fast in certain times of the year. Why, when Rei ignited the N^2 warhead against Zeruel, it was still daytime, and by the time Kaworu impaled Unit-01, it was already night, and i doubt that several hours passed between these two events, unless both Shinji and Zeruel got to the Geofront incredibly slowly.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Oct 29, 2011 12:14 pm SaltyJoe wrote: As for why didn't they try dropping bakelite: maybe they just don't have the infrastructure? I don't think Nerv had secret bakelite pumps installed on the road between the Geofront and the Test Site, and even they probably don't have an airborne vehicle that is big enough to carry sufficient quantites of bakelite, can be fired up quickly, and can drop said quantity in a relatively small target area. And i can't really think of any other means to try and trap it. What, lure it in an Eva-sized hole with a giant Scooby Snack? Throw a giant fishing net at it? And by the time Eva-03 is facing off with Eva-01, it's already too late. Bardiel was only slow moving while approaching (because it's menacing), but in combat, he could do the same lightning acrobatics as any Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sat Oct 29, 2011 2:08 pm bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: could bakelite even hold unit-03 at that point? I don't think so and given that we've seen unit-01 break through it with ease, I don't think it would be that much harder for Bardiel. I know that was EoE but still.

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Sat Oct 29, 2011 2:49 pm And i can't really think of any other means to try and trap it. What, lure it in an Eva-sized hole with a giant Scooby Snack? Throw a giant fishing net at it?

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Oct 29, 2011 3:36 pm Brainman wrote: Well the manga version shows Unit 01 being recaptured using a grappling hook system type thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Sat Oct 29, 2011 4:38 pm No, nothing about it's a big loss really. Technically speaking the scene works for what the story is supposed to be conveying (Shinji loses his faith in his father). Some of the scenes leading up to it were bothersome to me, but the scene itself works fine at a story level. This is just the typical, idle, "that's weird isn't it?" type thing. Because if you start asking too many practical questions you get into more trouble with it. All I'm saying is that this angel is uniquely stuck in the shape of an Eva, so if they could catch Jack Skelington they should be able to subdue this one too. At least long enough to extract the pilot (if they cared).

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Oct 29, 2011 6:50 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Sat Oct 29, 2011 7:54 pm ^ Yeah, well in the new one too Unit 01 puts it in its mouth . Ah, yes, a highly infectious life form that has the ability to take over the most advanced technology in the world on contact. I KNOW WHERE TO PUT THIS! OMPH! But again, the point of the scene is what it does to the characters (Shinji gets mad at Gendo). And when you open up that line of thinking eventually you have to apply it to all the angels. Such as Israphel. They establish that it can split in two when attacked. So they immediately formulate a plan that assumes both halves need to be destroyed at once. Well, how the hell do they know it isn't like a Hydra and can just keep splitting endlessly? Two becomes four, and then sixteen, etc etc. It ends up helping nobody. Edit: also, Bardiel might not be dead. We could have a "parasitic sidekick" character or adversary to look forward to in 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sun Oct 30, 2011 2:54 am Seele00TextOnly wrote: Since this is an infection type goo angel, how in the world does crushing it with a hand or mouth kill it INSTEAD of making you infected too?

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Sun Oct 30, 2011 8:20 am I don't know, maybe i'm just setting myself up to be exploited by lazy writers (though i don't really feel like i'm being exploited), but if two cases of somewhat similar events (Angels gone rouge) are handled differently by the narrative, isn't it easier to assume that there is an in-universe explanation for the differences? That is, Nerv just didn't have the means to get a hold of Unit-03?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Oct 30, 2011 1:14 pm SaltyJoe wrote: *Thoguh that could happen pretty fast in certain times of the year. Why, when Rei ignited the N^2 warhead against Zeruel, it was still daytime, and by the time Kaworu impaled Unit-01, it was already night, and i doubt that several hours passed between these two events, unless both Shinji and Zeruel got to the Geofront incredibly slowly.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sun Oct 30, 2011 1:53 pm Kendrix wrote: Rei blew up her missile inside the geofront. There are lights in the geofront.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Oct 30, 2011 8:25 pm ...
